title: Mechanical Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can generate first-pass CAD variants, run parametric FEA sweeps, and draft design calc reports, but stamped drawings, tolerance trade-offs, and prototype validation still require an engineer's sign-off.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generative-design add-ons inside SolidWorks, Fusion 360, and Ansys are shipping and improving quickly, but full autonomous design-to-manufacture loops are not yet standard.
rationales.adoption: Generative design and simulation copilots have reached large aerospace, automotive, and industrial-equipment OEMs but are still uneven at small and mid-size manufacturers.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run overnight simulation sweeps, flag out-of-tolerance results, and pre-populate BOMs, but physical prototyping and PE-stamped release remain human gates.

tasksAtRisk: Generating parametric CAD variants for early design exploration
Running routine finite element and thermal simulation sweeps
Drafting design calculation reports and bill-of-materials documentation
tasksGrowing: Physical prototype testing and failure investigation
Cross-disciplinary systems integration and trade-off judgment
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2141, p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI now runs simulation sweeps and drafts CAD variants overnight, compressing the early design loop. Mechanical engineers who own physical validation, manufacturability trade-offs, and final design sign-off stay indispensable.
